covered_sentences: So I chattered on, and gradually Heathcliff stopped frowning and began to smile.
Then we heard the Lintons' carriage arriving, and behind them the Earnshaws on their horses.
They always went to church on horseback.
Catherine brought Edgar and Isabella into the sitting room and set them down by the fire, which soon put a little color into their white faces.
I told Heathcliff to hurry into the sitting room and present himself, and he willingly obeyed.

covered_sentences: Unfortunately, as he entered the sitting room, he met Hindley, who pushed him back towards the kitchen, crying, "Joseph, keep Heathcliff out of the sitting room."
"Oh, sir," I said, "let him stay. He will be good."
"Go away, you vagabond! Look at this, how clean and elegant you are?"

covered_sentences: "I'll pull your hair till it is even longer."
"It's long enough already," said Edgar Linton. "It looks like a horse's mane."
Edgar did not mean to be insulting, but Heathcliff could not bear this remark from someone he already considered a rival.
He picked up a bowl of hot apple sauce and threw it over Edgar, who immediately burst into tears.
Catherine and Isabella ran over to his side.

covered_sentences: Hindley grabbed Heathcliff and took him into his room, where I have no doubt he hit him.
Because when Hindley returned, his face was red and his breathing heavy.
Isabella started weeping and saying that she wanted to go home.
Cathy stood there blushing for everyone.
She tried to cheer everyone up.
"Come, come," she said. "There is a delicious lunch waiting for us. Let's forget about this unfortunate incident and enjoy ourselves."
